mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward returns show no statistical significance during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that trigger immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of triggered months, or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises below 0.2pp.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a synchronized collapse in consumer and builder demand, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative returns when either series declines alone, or quarterly forward returns remain positive in over 60% of synchronized decline periods.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals weakening manufacturing output that reduces corporate earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over one month.
failure_condition: The signal produces positive forward returns during recovery phases, or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% when industrial production crosses below its trend.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate level above 4.5% combined with rising inflation expectations forces investors to demand higher equity risk premiums, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns within five trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 50% of periods when the rate exceeds 4.5%, or the signal shows no edge after controlling for inflation trends.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and falling real personal consumption expenditures indicates wage growth outpacing consumer spending power, creating inflationary pressure that suppresses S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive when payrolls rise and consumption falls, or the interaction term loses statistical significance in multivariate regressions.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly decline in real GDP growth below 1.0% signals an economic slowdown that reduces corporate profit forecasts and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 55% of quarters with sub-1% GDP growth, or the signal fails to predict returns during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month increases in the index of industrial production reflect strengthening manufacturing activity that boosts near-term earnings revisions and drives positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.01 over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns show no positive correlation with industrial production growth.
